headline: Introducing Serge: GitHub-Native AI Code Review
subject: 🚨 AI Alert: 93 new launches — Bloomberg Tech, CNBC Technology
article_title: Introducing Serge: GitHub-Native AI Code Review

article_items.title: Introducing Serge: GitHub-Native AI Code Review
article_items.description: A Blog post by Hugging Face on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by Hugging Face on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: Approval reviews are blocked by default unless ALLOW_APPROVE=1 is explicitly enabled. A Blog post by Hugging Face on Hugging Face Introducing Serge: GitHub-Native AI Code Review Serge is a GitHub-native code reviewer built around that constraint. If no model is configured, the reviewer can query the endpoint's /models route and fall back to the first returned model. Then it fetches the pull request, prepares the diff and repository context, calls the model, validates the result, and publishes the review. A reviewer can start a review, watch the model stream its work, edit or discard comments, and publish only the parts that are useful. If no model is configured, the reviewer can query the endpoint's /models route and fall back to the first returned model.
article_items.what_happened: Approval reviews are blocked by default unless ALLOW_APPROVE=1 is explicitly enabled. A Blog post by Hugging Face on Hugging Face Introducing Serge: GitHub-Native AI Code Review Serge is a GitHub-native code reviewer built around that constraint.
article_items.why_matters: If no model is configured, the reviewer can query the endpoint's /models route and fall back to the first returned model. Then it fetches the pull request, prepares the diff and repository context, calls the model, validates the result, and publishes the review.
article_items.what_to_watch: A reviewer can start a review, watch the model stream its work, edit or discard comments, and publish only the parts that are useful. If no model is configured, the reviewer can query the endpoint's /models route and fall back to the first returned model.
key_points: If no model is configured, the reviewer can query the endpoint's /models route and fall back to the first returned model.
Then it fetches the pull request, prepares the diff and repository context, calls the model, validates the result, and publishes the review.
In short: use the Action for a quick trial, the GitHub App for organization-wide automation, and the staged web app when you want humans to approve the model's comments before publishing.
Can teams choose the model they trust, including open models?
The staged web app is the human-in-the-loop path.
The Action is the fastest path: add an LLM API key as a repository secret, install the workflow, and comment on a pull request to start a review.
The Hugging Face Router is a strong fit: it exposes many open and commercial models through an OpenAI-compatible API, keeping the integration simple while giving operators real model choice.
The deployment modes also reflect GitHub's security model.
Teams should be able to run the model that fits their code, policy, cost, and deployment constraints, especially open models they can evaluate and swap over time.
Approval reviews are blocked by default unless ALLOW_APPROVE=1 is explicitly enabled.
It reviews pull requests with an OpenAI-compatible language model, follows repository-owned review rules, and publishes comments through GitHub's normal pull request review experience.
The default reviewer persona is "Serge".

article_items.title: OpenAI reportedly eyes IPO within a year as it readies new AI model and massive infrastructure push
